author: Tester <t@t.com>
body:
# Findings

`crates/jjf-storage/src/cache.rs` `rebuild_v3` used to silently
skip refs whose tip didn't resolve to a commit carrying the
expected blob. The blob-pointed-ref repro from the ticket was
reachable via `cat_blob`'s "absent path" translation
collapsing two distinct failure modes (tombstone vs. corrupt
ref) into one `Ok(None)` return — same problem on issue refs
and memory refs.

# Recommendation

Implemented the lighter fix: per-ref diagnostics captured into
`SnapshotCache::unreadable_refs`, exposed via
`Storage::unreadable_refs() -> Vec<UnreadableRef>`, surfaced by
`run_ls` / `run_ready` as a stderr warning. Object-type
discrimination is done up front via a new
`GitRepo::for_each_ref_with_type` probe — eliminates the
"blob-vs-tombstone" ambiguity at the source.

Files changed: `crates/jjf-storage/src/cache.rs`,
`crates/jjf-storage/src/lib.rs`, `crates/jjf-storage/src/git.rs`,
`crates/jjf-storage/src/v3_write.rs`, `crates/jjf/src/main.rs`,
`docs/cli-json.md`. Tests: 3 in
`crates/jjf-storage/tests/integration.rs`, 3 in
`crates/jjf/tests/ls.rs`, 1 in `crates/jjf/tests/ready.rs`.

Stdout shape preserved (bare array) under `--json`; warning
rides stderr as a one-line JSON envelope (no breaking change
for existing callers). Per-call cap of 5 inline ref names in
plain-text mode (`... and N more` tail beyond that).

Manual repro confirmed against the ticket's recipe in
`scratchpad/qa/corrupt-verify/`. 481/481 workspace tests
green.

# Confidence

High. The repro shape from the ticket body matched exactly;
the storage-layer test pins the surface; CLI tests pin the
operator-visible behavior including the `--json` stdout/stderr
split.

# Open follow-ups

The heavier `jjf doctor` verb (the ticket's primary fix
candidate) remains unfiled — file as a separate ticket if the
operator pull for "tell me about every malformed ref and how
to clean them" materializes. The lighter warning is enough
for now: it surfaces the casualty when the operator runs the
verb they were going to run anyway, and points naturally at
where a doctor would dig deeper. `list_issue_ids_v3` and
`list_memory_keys_v3` in `v3_write.rs` are now unused but kept
behind `#[allow(dead_code)]` — they'd be the natural primitive
for a future doctor verb.
